name: AWS Unused IP Addresses
short_description: Checks AWS for unused IP Addresses and, optionally, deletes them. See the [README](https://github.com/flexera-public/policy_templates/tree/master/cost/aws/unused_ip_addresses/) and [docs.flexera.com/flexera/EN/Automation](https://docs.flexera.com/flexera/EN/Automation/AutomationGS.htm) to learn more.

incident.summary_template: AWS Unused IP Addresses: {{ len data }} AWS Unused IP Addresses Found
incident.detail_template:
**Potential Monthly Savings:** {{ with index data 0 }}{{ .total_savings }}{{ end }}

 {{ with index data 0 }}{{ .message }}{{ end }}

incident.summary_template: AWS Unused IP Addresses: {{ len data }} Errors Identified
incident.detail_template:
The policy was unable to retrieve Elastic IP address data from one or more AWS regions due to permission or configuration errors.

 **Error Details:**
 {{ range data -}}
 - {{ .error }}
 {{ end -}}

 **Recommended Actions:**
 1. Verify that the AWS credential has the 'ec2:DescribeAddresses' permission for all regions where the policy should execute
 2. Ensure that the required AWS regions are enabled in your AWS account (some regions require explicit opt-in)
 3. Consider updating the 'Allow/Deny Regions List' parameter to exclude regions that are not accessible or not relevant to your use case

 **Additional Information:**
 If certain regions should be excluded from this policy's scope, you can configure the region filter parameters to prevent these errors from appearing in future policy runs.
